div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union (Steinhardt) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
1544. The Swedish Minister told me this afternoon that when Günther was in Helsinki on May 6, he received definite and positive assurances from Ryti, Witting and Walden that in the event of a German attack on the Soviet Union, Finland would remain neutral and “defend its neutrality against Germany if necessary”, and that the Finnish Government had given no notice to the Swedish Government
of its intended entry into the war against Soviet Union on the side of Germany. He said that the limited amount of food moving from Sweden to Finland since the outbreak of the Soviet-German war was being sent in response to German pressure.
Assarsson attributed the Finnish entry into the war to pressure on the Government from high Finnish military circles.
